description: Xilinx ZCU102 (Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, XCZU9EG) SUBSTRATE catalog — the boot/OS/hardware half of the layered profile model whose other half is the freq-plan operating-point catalog in `profiles.json`. A substrate fixes the board rev, FMC carrier + vcxo, Linux image (with fingerprints), boot-partition deploy, boot-mode switches, and the post-boot baseline (dmesg anchors + libiio device shape) BEFORE any freq-plan is applied. Substrate ⊥ freq-plan: a validated substrate composes with any compatible freq-plan from `profiles.json`. Unlike `profiles.json` (which is name-only — it points at patch/manifest data living in the consuming project), substrate entries here are SELF-CONTAINED machine-verifiable validation artifacts: image SHA256s, deploy MD5s, regex dmesg anchors, and the exact libiio baseline, so a runtime can assert a live board matches the substrate it was validated on. Human-readable boot/switch procedure for these substrates lives in the `zynq-boot` skill (references/ad9986-zcu102-kuiper-sdboot.md); this file is the executable counterpart.
fmc_daughter_cards_supported: ad9081-fmca-ebz (reference design; ADI Kuiper bundle ships the rev10-ad9081 board tree)
how_to_use: Select a substrate by id and compose it with a freq-plan from `profiles.json`. The substrate's `expected_dmesg_anchors` and `expected_libiio_baseline` are the pre-freq-plan acceptance gates; the freq-plan's own UART markers (see profiles.json) gate the JESD/datapath bring-up on top of the booted substrate. `validation_evidence` points back at the source thread + captured logs.

zcu102-ad9986-vcxo122p88-kuiper-2022r2p1.description: Validated Linux bring-up substrate: ZCU102 Rev1.0 + AD9986-FMCB-EBZ-W3 on FMC HPC0 + 122.88 MHz vcxo + Kuiper Linux 2022_R2 Patch1 with the m8_l4 BOOT.BIN and m8_l4_vcxo122p88 system.dtb. Validated by plan-01 of the 20260515 ZCU102 AD9986 linux bring-up thread; all 3 M0 acceptance gates PASS.

board.board_manual: Xilinx UG1182 — BUT see sw6.documentation_caveat below

sw6.documentation_caveat: Xilinx UG1182 Table 13-1 prescribes [0,1,1,1] (PS_MODE 1110, mode SD1-LS) for SD card boot on ZCU102. EMPIRICAL FINDING (plan-01): this Rev1.0 board boots SD from PS_MODE 0001 with switches [1,0,0,0] — confirmed by U-Boot's own 'Bootmode: LVL_SHFT_SD_MODE1' echo. The UG1182 setting causes BootROM to silently load a stale 2018.3 FSBL from an alternate boot source and stall at 'PMU-FW is not running'. ADI's AD9081 quickstart wiki has the correct setting in 'OFF,OFF,OFF,ON' notation. Always verify empirically via the U-Boot 'Bootmode:' line.

vadj.controlled_by: FMC EEPROM (auto-detected by ZCU102 VADJ control)

fmc_supply.rationale: AD9986 + HMC7044 SPI chip-selects assert correctly even with FMC unpowered, but MISO floats high and every register read returns 0xFF. Symptoms: 'hmc7044 spi2.0: Read/Write check failed (0x0)' + 'Probed, SPI read support failed' + 'ad9081 spi1.0: 8bit r/w test failed. Write 5a but readback is ff' + 'reset/init failed (-50)'. Linux still boots cleanly to login — board is alive but blind. Power FMC supply BEFORE powering the ZCU102.

carrier_chip.driver_identification_note: Linux kernel ad9081 driver prints 'Expected AD9081 found AD9986' at probe (informational, not error). Driver continues and probes the AD9986 successfully. Downstream consumers must use libiio context attribute hw_name=AD9986, not the device path basename 'axi-ad9081-{rx,tx}-hpc'.

fmc.vcxo_part_note: 122.88 MHz crystal oscillator on the FMC carrier. Documented as susceptible to a Kuiper 2023_R2 SPI boot bug (kernel-stage; does NOT manifest on 2022_R2_p1 — validated).

uart.bridge: Silicon Labs CP2108 Quad USB-to-UART (onboard ZCU102 debug)
uart.selector_note: Use SOCKS hil_lib.find_serial_port with board.preset=zcu102 + firmware.processor=psu_cortexa53_0 — auto-resolves to CP2108 interface 0 regardless of /dev/ttyUSB<N> reshuffle.

linux.uname_a: Linux analog 5.15.36-182044-g85078536473b #1140 SMP Wed Apr 3 07:20:38 EEST 2024 aarch64 GNU/Linux

boot_partition.kind: ADI Kuiper multi-board bundle (RPi DTBs + SoCFPGA + many Zynq/ZynqMP variants in per-board subdirectories; NO root-level Image/BOOT.BIN/system.dtb pre-deployed)

deploy_files.variant_note: ADI stock m8_l4 (M=8 L=4) BOOT.BIN. Boot header analysis confirms PMUFW correctly prepended (pmu_fw_total_length=129760 bytes at offset 0x38).
m8_l4 variant for 122.88 MHz vcxo (vs default 100 MHz vcxo). Required for HMC7044 to lock against the AD9986 carrier oscillator.
ADI Kuiper 2022_R2 common ZynqMP arm64 kernel. Shared across all zynqmp- projects.

uenv_overrides.note: Static IP via Linux kernel ipconfig (CONFIG_IP_PNP). Cosmetic quirk: IP-Config output shows gw=255.255.255.255 when gw field is blank — no actual default route installed, local-subnet routing works. For off-subnet routing add an explicit gateway.

rootfs_partition.preflight_note: SD card physical write-protect lock-tab MUST be in UNLOCK position; otherwise kernel detects (ro), rootfs mount with rw fails, EROFS, kernel panic. Six panics consumed before this was diagnosed in plan-01.

network.method: kernel ip= cmdline arg via BOOT/uEnv.txt::bootargs

preflight_checklist: SD card physical write-protect tab in UNLOCK position (slider toward contacts).
FMC HPC0 supply powered ON. Verify a power-indicator LED on the AD9986 carrier is lit BEFORE powering the ZCU102.
AD9986 carrier seated firmly on FMC HPC0; screws (if present) snug; no bent pins on FMC connector.
USB-UART cable: ZCU102 J83 → host. CP2108 enumerates as /dev/ttyUSB0..3 on the host (interface 0 = PS UART).

SW6 set to [SW6.1=ON, SW6.2=OFF, SW6.3=OFF, SW6.4=OFF]. Confirm at boot: U-Boot prints 'Bootmode: LVL_SHFT_SD_MODE1' within 5s of power-on. If you see 'Release 2018.3 Nov 8 2018' FSBL banner without PMUFW output, SW6 is wrong — flip SW6.1 to ON and the rest to OFF.
Optional for SSH: ethernet cable from ZCU102 J70 to a switch with a routable path to 192.168.0.x.
Optional: GNSS antenna into SMA on AD9986 carrier (RX1 input) — not required for M0; required for any subsequent GPS receiver tests.

expected_dmesg_anchors.pattern: Xilinx Zynq MP First Stage Boot Loader\s+Release 2022\.2\s+Feb\s+7 2024
expected_dmesg_anchors.evidence_line: Xilinx Zynq MP First Stage Boot Loader; Release 2022.2   Feb  7 2024  -  23:35:06

expected_dmesg_anchors.name: ad9986_probed
expected_dmesg_anchors.evidence_line: [    8.191299] ad9081 spi1.0: AD9986 Rev. 3 Grade 2 (API 1.6.0) probed

expected_dmesg_anchors.evidence_line: [    3.804226] axi_sysid 85000000.axi-sysid-0: [ad9081_fmca_ebz] on [zcu102] git branch <hdl_2022_r2> git <7ea497b6c24172ee293d6e0a9a387a16973da5ce> clean [2024-02-07 22:23:35] UTC

expected_dmesg_anchors.evidence_line: [    7.577895] macb ff0e0000.ethernet eth0: PHY [ff0e0000.ethernet-ffffffff:0c] driver [TI DP83867] (irq=POLL)

expected_dmesg_anchors.name: kuiper_login_prompt
expected_dmesg_anchors.pattern: Raspbian GNU/Linux 11 analog ttyPS0|Welcome to Kuiper GNU/Linux 11

context_attributes.hdl_system_id: [ad9081_fmca_ebz] on [zcu102] git branch [hdl_2022_r2] git [7ea497b6c24172ee293d6e0a9a387a16973da5ce] clean [2024-02-07 22:23:35] UTC

expected_libiio_baseline.baseline_note: This is the libiio shape immediately after substrate boot WITHOUT any freq-plan applied. ADC/DAC clocks, NCO frequencies, and JESD lane rates reflect ADI's stock m8_l4 reference (e.g. main_nco_frequency=400 MHz on RX channel voltage0_i, dac_frequency=12 GHz, sampling_frequency=250 MSPS). The GPS project's actual frequency plans (2048-quad-band-txm8l4 etc.) require custom bitstream + DTB and produce a different libiio shape — see the freq-plan profiles.

validation_evidence.verdict: M0 PASS (3/3 gates): Linux boots to userspace, AD9986 detected via libiio, NCO control via iio_attr verified read-side.
